title: INFINITY Surveys on Cultural Heritage & Reuse
content: The INFINITY project invites you to share your insights through two short surveys and contribute to shaping the future of the digital creative economy in Europe.
snippet: The INFINITY project invites you to share your insights through two short surveys and contribute to shaping the future of the digital creative economy in Europe.

title: Two PhD Positions in AI for Digital Heritage
content: The AI4DH project has launched an open call for two PhD positions, offering candidates the chance to contribute to cutting-edge research in the field of digital heritage.

title: Twin it! Campaign Grand Finale
content: This May marks an important milestone for Europe’s digital cultural heritage community: the grand finale of the “Twin it! 3D for Europe’s Culture” campaign.
snippet: This May marks an important milestone for Europe’s digital cultural heritage community: the grand finale of the “Twin it! 3D for Europe’s Culture” campaign.

title: ICARUS Convention #36
content: Participants are encouraged to submit contributions that reflect both practical experiences and research-driven approaches, highlighting how archival materials can be leveraged in the digital age.

title: A Week of European Collaboration Across Poznań, Riga, and Stubičke Toplice
content: The Time Machine Organisation played an active role in three major European events, engaging with partners, stakeholders and the wider cultural heritage community. From Poznań to Riga and Stubičke Toplice, last week emphasised the importance of collaboration, digital innovation and knowledge exchange in shaping the future of cultural heritage. Strengthening the Cultural Heritage Cloud in […]

title: Digital Ecosystems for Heritage 4.0
content: Researchers and practitioners working at the intersection of digital technologies and cultural heritage are invited to submit contributions to the symposium Digital Ecosystems for Heritage 4.0 Artificial Intelligence, Values and Risk Assessment for Conservation, taking place from 26-28 August in Florence, Italy.

title: FAIR 3D HERITAGE Conference
content: The FAIR 3D HERITAGE Conference will take place in Mainz, Germany, from 14 to 16 September 2026. The conference invites researchers, practitioners, and professionals working at the intersection of cultural heritage and digital technologies to contribute to an important discussion on the future of 3D heritage. As 3D digitisation becomes increasingly central to cultural heritage […]

title: Connective Holocaust Commemoration Design Sprints 2026
content: The Landecker Digital Memory Lab at the University of Sussex has launched its 2026 Connective Holocaust Commemoration Design Sprints, inviting professionals from across disciplines and sectors to collaboratively develop prototype solutions for challenges in digital Holocaust memory.

title: On the Ground at the 3D-4CH Winter School 2026
content: TMO joined on-site at the Art & History Museum in Brussels, where participants from across Europe gathered for the first 3D-4CH Winter School. Over three days, heritage professionals came together to explore the transformative potential of 3D technologies in museums and cultural heritage, from cutting-edge digitisation techniques to immersive XR storytelling. We witnessed participants dive […]

title: Rediscovering İzmir’s Historic Hans
content: The İzmir Time Machine is bringing the city’s historic han buildings back into public memory through digital reconstruction and an interactive mapping platform.
snippet: The İzmir Time Machine is bringing the city’s historic han buildings back into public memory through digital reconstruction and an interactive mapping platform.
